MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Andrew McClelland; Lunksi & Stimpert LLP; 415-426-3525; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Christophe Baubert and Aleyda Baubert.
Michele M. Tuman; Shannon B. Jones Law Group, Inc.; 925-837-2317; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Side, Inc. dba Harper Real Estate and Loukia Karneris.
Peter B. Fredman; Law Office of Peter Fredman PC; 510-868-2626; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Veronica Stork.

Plaintiffs contest the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHE BAUBERT, ALEYDA BAUBERT MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Assuming without deciding that a party against whom no claim is alleged or relief sought in a pleading is permitted to seek the striking of a portion of the pleading, the Bauberts have failed to show sufficient grounds to strike any of the portions of Ms. Stork's cross-complaint that they seek to be stricken. Evidence Code 1152 and 1154 do not support the Bauberts' motion for two reasons. First, those sections pertain to admissibility of evidence and, unlike the sealing rules, do not establish a basis to strike any pleading language. Second, those sections only make settlement information inadmissible for specified subjects, not for all purposes, and the references to the June 22, 2020 demand letter are not made for any of those prohibited purposes. The references to the demand letter are, at a minimum, relevant to Ms. Stork's indemnity claim against Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Andrew McClelland; Lunksi & Stimpert LLP; 415-426-3525; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Christophe Baubert and Aleyda Baubert.
Michele M. Tuman; Shannon B. Jones Law Group, Inc.; 925-837-2317; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Side, Inc. dba Harper Real Estate and Loukia Karneris.
Peter B. Fredman; Law Office of Peter Fredman PC; 510-868-2626; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Veronica Stork.

Defendants Side, Inc. dba Harper Real Estate and Loukia Karneris contest the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT SIDE, INC., LOUKIA KARNERIS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action. While they could have been more clearly alleged, all causes of action for elder abuse, breach of contract and UCL violation are adequately alleged. The elder abuse claim sufficiently alleges financial elder abuse based on the taking of money from Ms. Stork with the intent to defraud and Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris knew or should have known that their failure to disclose the encroachment problem to the Bauberts would likely be harmful to Ms. Stork. The breach of contract claim sufficiently alleges a breach of the portion of the parties' contract requiring Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris to "exercise reasonable effort and due diligence to achieve the purposes of this Agreement." The UCL claim sufficiently alleges a viable claim based on the fraud prong of that statute and seeks the available remedy of restitution of some or all of the commission Ms. Stork paid to Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
